hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 residues (e.g., Y193, F205), while avoiding off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. This should increase selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 binding and reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: strong SSTR2 binding and selectivity can be achieved by optimizing non-pharmacophore positions. However, structural quality must be improved to ensure viable candidates.

proposed_changes.rationale: Candidates are failing due to poor structural quality (pLDDT=0.0, dock=0.00), likely due to excessive clashes. Reducing the clash tolerance will enforce better geometry and improve structural quality.

To further improve selectivity, we should focus on mutating non-pharmacophore positions (1/2/5/6/11/12) to disrupt conserved-pocket contacts with SSTR3/SSTR5 while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
